parsing_res_list.block_content: Thirteen Fallaciesabout the Users and Uses of EIL
Native English as the norm and native speakers as norm providers:linguacentric and ethnocentric attitudes

1A linguistic chauvinism,or if you will,ethnocentricity,is SO deeply rooted, not only in British culture,but also in the minds and hearts of a large number of language teachers working abroad,that many of the people who embrace such bias find it difficult to accept that other varieties of English,for some learners,are better choices for the educational model in the teaching of English as a foreign or second language Modiano,1999)

core_content.markdown:
### 8. Conclusion 

Native English as the norm and native speakers as norm providers:linguacentric and ethnocentric attitudes 

1A linguistic chauvinism,or if you will,ethnocentricity,is SO deeply rooted, not only in British culture,but also in the minds and hearts of a large number of language teachers working abroad,that many of the people who embrace such bias find it difficult to accept that other varieties of English,for some learners,are better choices for the educational model in the teaching of English as a foreign or second language Modiano,1999)
